title: The neural basis of metacognitive monitoring during arithmetic in the developing brain.

assets.abstract: In contrast to a substantial body of research on the neural basis of cognitive performance in several academic domains, less is known about how the brain generates metacognitive (MC) awareness of such performance. The existing work on the neurobiological underpinnings of metacognition has almost exclusively been done in adults and has largely focused on lower level cognitive processing domains, such as perceptual decision-making. Extending this body of evidence, we investigated MC monitoring by

phase_mapping.bridge_rationale: Metacognition — self-monitoring, metacognitive awareness, prefrontal-parietal insight circuits.
